case6_special-population_intermediate_ol.blueprintNote: Best available matches were advanced in the blueprint. Generated at advanced complexity but slotted in case 6 as closest population match. Documented in blueprintRevisionRecommendations.

L2_interpretation.noteOnCount: 42 IDs listed to cross-reference; final authoritative count is 40 per programmatic verification

L3_action.noteOnCount: 28 IDs listed to cross-reference; final authoritative count is 30 per programmatic verification

distributionAudit.verificationMethod: Programmatic JSON parse via Python — python3 counted cognitiveLayer field on all 80 question objects

dispositionCoverage.note: All high-risk topic questions embed disposition reasoning in stem, options, or rationale regardless of tag presence. Tagged questions represent explicit multi-option disposition choices.

distractorQualityAudit.methodology: All distractors represent documented clinical error patterns
errorTypesRepresented: Premature reassurance: UTI-004 (treating bacteriuria as UTI), KP-006 (ordering knee MRI for hip pain)
Over-escalation: HTN-001 (ED transfer for urgency), UTI-004 option C (ciprofloxacin for non-UTI delirium)
Under-recognition of red flags: HA-003 option A (triptan for PComm aneurysm), CA-002 option A (reassurance for hematuria)
Wrong medication for comorbidity: T2DM-002 (saxagliptin in HFrEF), DEPR-005 option C (adding AChEI to anticholinergic)
Incorrect monitoring interval: GER-005 (eGFR vs CrCl dosing error)
Inappropriate antibiotic choice: UTI-001 (FQ for uncomplicated cystitis), COPD-004 (moxifloxacin as first-line)
Population mismatch: PREP-003 option A (Descovy for vaginal exposure), SMK-005 option D (varenicline in pregnancy)
Guideline-inconsistent next step: HTN-002 option C (vasodilator alone in aortic dissection), COPD-005 option A (increasing O2)
Sublingual nifedipine danger: HTN-001 option B (explicitly tested as a known dangerous error)

advanced_distinguishing_features: T2DM: SGLT-2 comorbidity selection matrix (HFrEF vs ASCVD vs CKD), euglycemic DKA recognition, frailty-based A1c individualization, pre-op drug management
COPD: eosinophil-guided ICS addition, CO2 retention oxygen targeting, device selection for functional decline, admission criteria
Headache: PComm aneurysm CN3 palsy recognition, GCA same-day steroid initiation, MOH pattern recognition, immunocompromised meningitis sequence
Syncope: HOCM in young athletes, QTc-prolonging drug crisis, LBBB in HFrEF syncope admission decision
PrEP: Descovy vaginal restriction, seroconversion management protocol, trans-specific regimen, anatomy-based history
Geriatric: Cockcroft-Gault vs eGFR dosing, frailty-adjusted A1c targets, anticoagulation in dementia shared decision
Deprescribing: triple whammy cascade, benzodiazepine taper protocol, anticholinergic cascade recognition
intermediate_distinguishing_features: Knee OA: first-line vs escalation therapy, steroid injection timing, Ottawa rules
UTI: uncomplicated vs complicated classification, stewardship-guided antibiotic, ASB in pregnancy vs elderly
HTN: urgency vs emergency distinction, resistant HTN 4th agent, orthostatic titration in elderly
Cancer: ALARM criteria, 2-week urgency referral, USPSTF LDCT thresholds
Psoriasis/AD: PASI/DLQI thresholds, topical potency site-matching, eczema herpeticum emergency recognition

materialChanges: PREP-003: Descovy NOT approved for vaginal exposure — directly changed correct answer from Descovy to Truvada
PREP-002: Transgender woman regimen selection — injectable cabotegravir preferred for adherence challenges in this population
PREP-006: Anatomy-based vs identity-based history — correct answer requires inclusive communication approach
PREP-007: Site-specific STI screening (rectal + urethral + pharyngeal) — changed monitoring requirement
GER-005: Cockcroft-Gault CrCl 27 vs eGFR 44 — correct answer changes dosing tier completely
GER-004: Anticoagulation in dementia with falls — introduces shared decision-making rather than algorithmic answer
DEPR-005: Oxybutynin → mirabegron switch — anticholinergic burden drives correct answer
HTN-006: BP target relaxed from <130/80 to accommodate frailty and orthostasis
UTI-004: Do NOT treat ASB as UTI in elderly with altered mental status — changed recommended action
UTI-003: Pregnancy reverses ASB management — treat all positive cultures regardless of symptoms
UTI-003: TMP-SMX specifically contraindicated in pregnancy — changed correct antibiotic answer
SMK-005: NRT preferred in pregnancy; varenicline safety data insufficient — materially changed cessation recommendation
All high-risk urgent-care questions include explicit disposition (ED/immediate/outpatient) as correct or distractor answer options
HA-003: PComm aneurysm requires 911 call, not ophthalmology or MRI
HTN-003: Eclampsia requires magnesium + 911, not oral antihypertensives
T2DM-002: HFrEF comorbidity directly changed correct agent from GLP-1 to SGLT-2
T2DM-003: ASCVD + CKD changed agent selection and contraindicated glyburide (accumulated metabolites)
COPD-003: Eosinophil count 380 changed answer from 'no ICS' to triple therapy

metadataConsistencyCheck.verificationStatus: PASSED — all counts match across all three files

blueprintRevisionRecommendations.issue: Case 6 gap: no clean special-population + intermediate + older-adult topic available
blueprintRevisionRecommendations.detail: Both geriatric-assessment-falls-polypharmacy-frailty and deprescribing-beers-criteria-older-adults are classified as 'advanced' in the blueprint. No intermediate older-adult topic existed. These were slotted in case6 as closest available matches and generated at their blueprint complexity.
blueprintRevisionRecommendations.recommendation: Add a dedicated intermediate older-adult topic to the blueprint. Suggested: 'osteoporosis-screening-and-bisphosphonate-initiation' — covers DEXA screening indications, FRAX scoring, bisphosphonate prescribing (alendronate weekly vs IV zoledronate annual), calcium/vitamin D supplementation, and 3-5 year drug holiday — aligns with outpatient NP scope and older-adult population context.

blueprintRevisionRecommendations.detail: The blueprint classifies prep-hiv-prevention-prescribing-monitoring as 'intermediate' in careComplexity. The actual content — Descovy vaginal exposure restriction, seroconversion management protocol, injectable cabotegravir selection, anatomy-based communication, and site-specific monitoring requirements — is advanced in complexity.
blueprintRevisionRecommendations.recommendation: Upgrade blueprint careComplexity for prep-hiv-prevention-prescribing-monitoring from 'intermediate' to 'advanced' to accurately reflect content requirements.

blueprintRevisionRecommendations.detail: No topic in the selected batch was tagged with behavioral-health as a primary population modifier. Mental health comorbidity is clinically relevant across many topics (smoking cessation in depression, PrEP in substance use, anxiety in hypertension) but was addressed only tangentially.
blueprintRevisionRecommendations.recommendation: Add a dedicated topic: 'depression-in-primary-care-collaborative-care' with behavioral-health population tag and intermediate complexity, covering PHQ-9 thresholds, antidepressant initiation, and collaboration triggers.

blueprintRevisionRecommendations.detail: DOAC prescribing (apixaban, rivaroxaban) for AF appears across multiple topics (syncope, geriatrics) but is not a standalone blueprint topic with full dosing, renal adjustment, and monitoring detail.
blueprintRevisionRecommendations.recommendation: Add: 'doac-management-in-af-and-vte-outpatient' at advanced complexity with chronic-disease and older-adult population tags.
